title: Margin of Safety
event: Reyes Co. expects sales of $400,000; its break-even point is $300,000 of sales. Compute the margin of safety in dollars and as a percent.

computationPaths.narration: Margin of safety measures the cushion: how far sales can fall before losses begin.

memorize.body: MoS = expected sales − break-even sales. MoS % = MoS ÷ EXPECTED sales.
The percent divides by EXPECTED sales, not break-even sales — a 25% margin means sales can drop a quarter before red ink.

questions.prompt: Expected sales $400,000, break-even $300,000. Margin of safety percent = ?

distractors.feedback: You divided by BREAK-EVEN sales. The base is EXPECTED sales: 100 ÷ 400 = 25%.
That's break-even as a % of sales. The margin of SAFETY is the cushion above it: 25%.
